test.create_query: CREATE TABLE build_wide (k UInt64, a UInt64, b UInt64, c UInt64, d UInt64, e UInt64, f UInt64, g UInt64) ENGINE = MergeTree ORDER BY tuple()
CREATE TABLE build_narrow (k UInt64, a UInt64) ENGINE = MergeTree ORDER BY tuple()

CREATE TABLE build_nullable (k UInt64, a Nullable(UInt64), b Nullable(UInt64), c Nullable(UInt64), d Nullable(UInt64), e Nullable(UInt64), f Nullable(UInt64), g Nullable(UInt64)) ENGINE = MergeTree ORDER BY tuple()
CREATE TABLE probe (k1 UInt64, k2 UInt64, k3 UInt64) ENGINE = MergeTree ORDER BY tuple()
test.fill_query: INSERT INTO build_wide SELECT number, number, number, number, number, number, number, number FROM numbers(10000000) ORDER BY rand()
INSERT INTO build_narrow SELECT number, number FROM numbers(10000000) ORDER BY rand()
INSERT INTO build_nullable WITH if(number % 10 = 0, NULL, number) AS n SELECT number, n, n, n, n, n, n, n FROM numbers(10000000) ORDER BY rand()
INSERT INTO probe SELECT number + 9000000, number + 5000000, number + 1000000 FROM numbers(11000000) ORDER BY rand()
test.query: SELECT * FROM probe p RIGHT JOIN build_wide b ON p.{probe_key} = b.k FORMAT Null
SELECT * FROM probe p RIGHT JOIN build_narrow b ON p.{probe_key} = b.k FORMAT Null
SELECT * FROM probe p RIGHT JOIN build_nullable b ON p.{probe_key} = b.k FORMAT Null
SELECT * FROM probe p FULL JOIN build_wide b ON p.{probe_key} = b.k FORMAT Null
SELECT * FROM probe p FULL JOIN build_narrow b ON p.{probe_key} = b.k FORMAT Null
SELECT * FROM probe p FULL JOIN build_nullable b ON p.{probe_key} = b.k FORMAT Null
